messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this section of a document.
Focus on what questions this section can answer: topics, entities, dates,
definitions, procedures, claims, tables, figures, metrics, and evidence types.
Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.
This is text window 1 of 3 for the section.

Section metadata:
{
  "node_id": "0007",
  "title": "Cross-Platform Publishing",
  "page_index": 8,
  "page_range": "8-10"
}

Section text:
Cross-Platform Publishing
According to a 2024 U.S.-focused study by Bain &
Co., as many as 70% of the gamers surveyed said
they played on multiple devices and asked for more
ubiquity in the future. Another American market
study by the Consumer Technology Association
(CTA) involving over 2,700 adults and teens, found
this number to stand at 61%. The last few years
have witnessed robust growth in the number
of multiplatform game launches, with gaming
company Unity pegging this at around 40% between
2021 and 2023.
Game Publishing Strategies
SECTION 1
Digital distribution through
online platforms such as Steam
and Epic Games Store has
lowered or outright obliterated the
barriers for developers to self-
publish their games. Steam sold
over 718 million games in 2024
alone, while Epic Games Store
boasted annual PC user spending
of US$950 million in 2023.
7
7
Book Online
Visit Site
Visit Site
The Global State of Game Publishing and Marketing 2025 Industry Report
Cross-platform development and publishing
involves designing games in a way that they can
provide a consistent user experience across various
devices such as PCs, consoles, and mobile phones,
with minimal to no adjustments. Even though
hardware limitations could affect the graphics and
overall gameplay smoothness, the architecture and
overall experience remain consistent.
This strategy allows gamers to connect and play
with others regardless of their device, fostering a
unified player base that drives engagement, long-
term player retention, and consequently higher
revenues, with estimates pegging it at around
40%. There are over three billion active gamers
worldwide, and the market is expected to value
almost US$151 billion by 2030. Cross-platform games
allow developers access to this vast, multi-platform
pool of potential gamers. Modern players frequently
switch between devices, and cross-platform
compatibility enables them to purchase a game
on one system and seamlessly access it across
multiple platforms.
There are over three billion active
gamers worldwide, and the
market is expected to value US$151
billion by 2030. Cross-platform
games allow developers access to
this vast, multi-platform pool of
potential gamers. Modern players
frequently switch between
devices, and cross-platform
compatibility enables them to
purchase a game on one system
and seamlessly access it across
multiple platforms.
FIGURE 2
Share of Players and Time Spent on Playing
Note: Total number of players in the survey, n=58,040
Source: Newzoo
Share of players
Played on
one platform
Avergae time spent
playing per week
Played on
two platforms
Played on
three platforms
4h
8h 24m
11h 18m
41%
85%
69%
8
8
Book Online
Visit Site
Visit Site
The Global State of Game Publishing and Marketing 2025 Industry Report
Benefits
Descriptions
Wider audience reach
Accessible to more players which increases revenue potential for developers and publishers.
From a developer’s perspective, it is more cost-
efficient to create cross-platform games due to
a reduction in the need for multiple specialised
teams, resources required to develop and test on
different platforms, and the creation of a single
codebase. Cross-platform gaming also gives
developers and publishers a better understanding
of players and greater coherency for advertising,
promotions, and analytics.
Moreover, players who utilize multiple platforms
also tend to exhibit higher levels of engagement.
A study by gaming data provider Newzoo found
that gamers who play on three platforms average
11 hours and 18 minutes of gameplay per week,
compared to just 4 hours for those restricted to a
single platform.
Therefore, even companies like Sony and Microsoft,
which have traditionally developed single-platform
games only, are now shifting their focus away from
exclusivity in favour of broader cross-platform
strategies. The economic advantages of reaching a
larger audience often outweigh the opportunity costs
of hardware sales (of PlayStations and Xbox), especially
as rising development costs make it increasingly
challenging to achieve profitability on a single platform.
Key benefits of developing a cross-platform game
include:
TABLE 1
Benefits of Developing Cross-Platform Games
Source: AgileIntel
Cost and time efficiency
Allows studios to develop games faster and at lower costs. Instead of building a separate version of
the game for each platform, developers can use a single codebase with minor tweaks for platform-
specific requirements.
Reduces the time spent in development and testing, allowing studios to allocate resources to other
critical areas.
Easier maintenance
and updates
With a unified codebase, managing updates and bug fixes becomes simpler.
Updates can be deployed across all platforms simultaneously.
Keeps the gaming experience consistent for all players and simplifies post-launch maintenance.
Better player
engagement
Cross-platform games allow players to interact and compete with others regardless of their device.

message.content:
**Topics:** Cross-platform game publishing and development, digital distribution ecosystems, player engagement and retention, market growth and valuation, development cost efficiency, industry strategy shifts away from platform exclusivity.

**Entities:** Bain & Co., Consumer Technology Association (CTA), Unity, Steam, Epic Games Store, Newzoo, Sony, Microsoft, AgileIntel.

**Dates/Timeframes:** 2021–2023, 2023, 2024, 2030 (market forecast).

**Definitions:** Cross-platform development/publishing: Designing games to deliver a consistent user experience across PCs, consoles, and mobile devices with minimal adjustments, maintaining unified architecture and gameplay despite hardware-specific limitations.

**Claims:** Multi-platform gaming is highly prevalent and increasingly demanded by players; cross-platform compatibility unifies player bases, drives long-term retention, and increases revenue (estimated ~40% uplift); traditional console manufacturers (Sony, Microsoft) are shifting from strict exclusivity to cross-platform strategies due to rising development costs and the economic advantages of broader audience reach; single-codebase development reduces costs, testing time, and simplifies post-launch maintenance.

**Tables & Figures:** Table 1 outlines key benefits of cross-platform development (wider audience reach, cost/time efficiency, easier maintenance/updates, better player engagement). Figure 2 presents survey data correlating the number of platforms played on with player share and average weekly playtime.

**Metrics:** 70% of gamers play on multiple devices (Bain & Co., 2024); 61% (CTA study, n>2,700); ~40% of game launches were multiplatform (Unity, 2021–2023); 718M games sold on Steam (2024); $950M annual PC user spending on Epic Games Store (2023); >3B active gamers globally; ~$151B projected market value by 2030; ~40% revenue/retention increase attributed to cross-platform play; weekly playtime averages: 4h (1 platform), 8h 24m (2 platforms), 11h 18m (3 platforms) (Newzoo, n=58,040).

**Evidence Types:** Industry market research reports, platform sales/financial disclosures, developer ecosystem analytics, large-scale player behavior surveys, market forecasts, and structured analytical frameworks.

**Procedures/Workflows:** Utilizing a single codebase with minor platform-specific tweaks; deploying simultaneous updates and bug fixes across all platforms; streamlining testing and resource allocation to reduce development cycles.

**Answerable Retrieval Questions:** What defines cross-platform game publishing and development? How prevalent is multi-platform gaming among consumers? What are the financial, developmental, and maintenance benefits of cross-platform games? How does playing on multiple devices impact weekly engagement time? What are the current sales and spending metrics for major PC distribution platforms? How are legacy console manufacturers adapting to cross-platform trends? What is the projected global gaming market size and active player count?

response_text:
**Topics:** Cross-platform game publishing and development, digital distribution ecosystems, player engagement and retention, market growth and valuation, development cost efficiency, industry strategy shifts away from platform exclusivity.

**Entities:** Bain & Co., Consumer Technology Association (CTA), Unity, Steam, Epic Games Store, Newzoo, Sony, Microsoft, AgileIntel.

**Dates/Timeframes:** 2021–2023, 2023, 2024, 2030 (market forecast).

**Definitions:** Cross-platform development/publishing: Designing games to deliver a consistent user experience across PCs, consoles, and mobile devices with minimal adjustments, maintaining unified architecture and gameplay despite hardware-specific limitations.

**Claims:** Multi-platform gaming is highly prevalent and increasingly demanded by players; cross-platform compatibility unifies player bases, drives long-term retention, and increases revenue (estimated ~40% uplift); traditional console manufacturers (Sony, Microsoft) are shifting from strict exclusivity to cross-platform strategies due to rising development costs and the economic advantages of broader audience reach; single-codebase development reduces costs, testing time, and simplifies post-launch maintenance.

**Tables & Figures:** Table 1 outlines key benefits of cross-platform development (wider audience reach, cost/time efficiency, easier maintenance/updates, better player engagement). Figure 2 presents survey data correlating the number of platforms played on with player share and average weekly playtime.

**Metrics:** 70% of gamers play on multiple devices (Bain & Co., 2024); 61% (CTA study, n>2,700); ~40% of game launches were multiplatform (Unity, 2021–2023); 718M games sold on Steam (2024); $950M annual PC user spending on Epic Games Store (2023); >3B active gamers globally; ~$151B projected market value by 2030; ~40% revenue/retention increase attributed to cross-platform play; weekly playtime averages: 4h (1 platform), 8h 24m (2 platforms), 11h 18m (3 platforms) (Newzoo, n=58,040).

**Evidence Types:** Industry market research reports, platform sales/financial disclosures, developer ecosystem analytics, large-scale player behavior surveys, market forecasts, and structured analytical frameworks.

**Procedures/Workflows:** Utilizing a single codebase with minor platform-specific tweaks; deploying simultaneous updates and bug fixes across all platforms; streamlining testing and resource allocation to reduce development cycles.

**Answerable Retrieval Questions:** What defines cross-platform game publishing and development? How prevalent is multi-platform gaming among consumers? What are the financial, developmental, and maintenance benefits of cross-platform games? How does playing on multiple devices impact weekly engagement time? What are the current sales and spending metrics for major PC distribution platforms? How are legacy console manufacturers adapting to cross-platform trends? What is the projected global gaming market size and active player count?
